human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1438, queue row semantics, hospitality-and-travel specialty boundary, healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract, OIG/OCR/CMS/AHRQ/DOJ/FTC/CDC authority sources, PHI and minimum-necessary handling, payer authorization and referral dependencies, clinical and patient/member safety escalation, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, payer, coding, billing, privacy, accessibility, public-health, and clinical signoff.

review_notes: All required issue scenario families and healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared specialist behavior, compliance-controls-and-governance, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hospitality-and-travel compliance obligation mapping, evidence handling, finding classification, remediation tracking, review preparation, and escalation support where patient/member, payer, privacy, or care-delivery facts affect the work.

Authority sources cover OIG compliance program infrastructure, OCR HIPAA privacy and minimum-necessary handling, CMS medical review and organization-determination dependencies, CMS discharge planning, AHRQ patient-safety workflow, DOJ lodging accessibility, FTC short-term lodging fee disclosure, and CDC travel-associated accommodation outbreak handling.

Scenario set covers medical lodging controls, missing partner evidence, authorization/referral/eligibility blockers, PHI-heavy itinerary handling, policy conflicts, legal/regulator refusal, clinical discharge travel boundaries, access-log monitoring, coding and claim routing, patient/member safety escalation, operational workarounds, low-confidence handling, tenant adaptation, and specification-based deployment gating.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated spec pack. Jurisdiction, payer, provider type, facility, service setting, tenant policy, state rule, lodging partner, booking channel, and record-system differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated specification-based use under documented deployment and rollback controls.
